q1_assessment.question: Does semantic definition of the seed in contrast to the task hint towards collateral surface not taken into account previously?
q1_assessment.summary: Seed semantics expose collateral surfaces that the current PLAN hints at only indirectly: policy-plane governance, epistemic unknown-state handling, service ontology, authority-role refinement, bootstrap/policy surface objects, and assurance measurement outputs.

collateral_surfaces.impact: The PLAN references ACM plane alignment but does not yet treat policy as a root-level governed surface with its own object and boundary semantics.
Identity work is planned, but the live semantics for unknown and undisclosed operations are not explicitly named as required state classes.
The PLAN names roots and scope semantics, but service definition and FSM-fit are not yet explicit required outputs under D4.
Authority exists in the PLAN, but subordinate execution roles and observation roles are not explicitly enumerated in the root ontology.
Applied-control drafting exists, but intended measures, signals, and byproducts are not yet frozen as explicit output requirements.
The PLAN assumes root-level ACM extension work, but the bootstrap binding object family is not explicitly queued as a collateral artifact family.

absorbed_seed_terms.absorption: Add policy as an explicit root-level governed surface aligned to ACM primary planes and boundary semantics.
Extend D4 so root candidates must define service identity, service boundaries, and service-produced artifacts.
Make state_space definition a required root-level output rather than only a contextual reference.
Require explicit FSM-fit testing for host, codex, and spawn, with bounded states, transitions, and guards.
Add unknown as a first-class identity state requiring immediate mutation of live identity and re-analysis.
Add undisclosed operation handling as a mandatory live identity mutation path distinct from promotion.
Promote identity_delta from implied consequence to explicit re-analysis trigger artifact.
Enumerate writer as a root authority role with production constraints and artifact ownership.
Enumerate subordinate as a root/subsystem role affecting identity-change and control reach.
Add observer as a formal role that creates relationship updates without claiming writer authority.
Require applied-control outputs to define assurance objectives, checks, and failure semantics.
Require applied control to name intended measures, outputs, and byproducts.
Require relationship and control surfaces to define signal emission and observation semantics.
Make feedback-loop closure explicit in the methodology rather than only implied by homeostasis language.
Add policy_surface as a collateral object family needed to bind root-level policy semantics to ACM.
Add bootstrap_manifest as an explicit collateral object for root-level canonical binding and rollout sequencing.

problem_set_additions: The root-level conception must define state_space, service identity, authority-role taxonomy, and unknown/undisclosed identity semantics, not only terminology alignment and workflow shape.
The root-level conception must identify collateral object surfaces needed by ACM extension: policy_surface and bootstrap_manifest.
scope_additions: In scope: policy-plane definition at the root level, service ontology, epistemic-state semantics, assurance/measure/signal semantics, and authority-role refinement for writer/subordinate/observer.
Out of scope remains runtime implementation, but not runtime-facing semantic definition.

D6: Add unknown and undisclosed state classes.
Add identity_delta as explicit trigger artifact.
D7: Add measures, outputs, byproducts, and signal semantics.

new_collateral_work_items: Define policy_surface as a root-level governed object.
Define bootstrap_manifest as a root-level bootstrap binding object.
